ground_truth.title: The Top 25 Hosting Trends of 2025, According to Event Planners
ground_truth.author: Rebecca Norris
ground_truth.main_content: Summer is officially here, and with it, endless potential to host the soiree of your dreams. While classic backyard barbecues, whimsical midsummer parties, and elevated alfresco gatherings are common themes this time of year, there’s always the option to think more creatively to host a curated event that guests will remember long after last call. An easy way to get the ball rolling is to brush up on the top hosting trends of 2025. To help you do so, we chatted with some of the industry’s top event planners to uncover the details that make any party stand out. “This year, it’s less about showing off and more about creating a sense of ease,” says Beth Helmstetter, founder and creative director of Beth Helmstetter Events. “The best events feel intentional, beautiful, and personal.” Another way to look at it? Through the attendees’ eyes. “What are your guests going to remember? Hosts want their guests to be comfortable, to be entertained, and to be taken care of,” says celebrity party planner Mindy Weiss, founder of Mindy Weiss Party Consultants. Ahead, peruse 25 hosting trends bound to make your special event that much more memorable. A Focus on Storytelling What story do you want your event to tell? “This year, it’s not just about beautiful design, it’s about meaning,” says Estera Events Co-Owner and Lead Planner Erica Vanco. “From invitations to tablescapes, hosts are weaving in personal narratives that connect guests to place, memory, or culture. Whether it's referencing a childhood dish, using heirloom decor for the tablescape, or naming cocktails after shared moments, storytelling transforms an event from stylish to deeply personal.” Customized Menu and Paper Suites A memorable way to set the tone for your party is with interactive paper suites, encompassing everything from the invitation and RSVP card to the name care and menu. “We love to enhance guest experience with our paper suites,” says Holloway Events Founder and Lead Designer Cathleen Holloway. “A gorgeous color palette with custom shapes previewing the amazing night ahead. Once, we created an interactive oyster plate menu for each wedding guest, which doubled as a placecard as well. Behind each oyster, [we] listed out each course. Guests are still talking about it to this day!” Neo-Pastel Palettes with Saturated Accents When it comes to the color story of your event, you have a few options. Up first, we have modern pastels with a pop of vibrant color. “Gone are the saccharine pastels of the past; 2025 sees the rise of sophisticated, dusty pastels grounded by bold, unexpected accent colors,” says Lara Mahler, founder of The Privilege is Mine. “Think: sage with ochre, dusty lilac with marigold, or powder blue with deep wine.” A Full Drip Color Story Party color palettes don’t have to be subdued in 2025. In fact, many hosts are opting for deeply saturated color stories. “Clients aren't being shy about their desired color palettes these days,” Holloway says. “Just like we're seeing in the interior design space, our clients are completely leaning into a ‘full drip,’ almost monochromatic palette, for their weddings.” Bold Color Mixes If monochromatic color palettes aren’t your thing, turn to the boldest color mixes of the year. “2025 is all about fearless color—rich shades like red, chocolate brown, punchy pinks, lavender, yellow, cobalt, and chartreuse are dominating design palettes,” says Estera Events Co-Owner and Lead Planner Bianca Hall. “The key to keeping this sophisticated is layering saturated hues with wood tones, metallics, or stone to balance drama with elegance. It’s confident, expressive, and anything but boring.” A Moment for Chartreuse If you want to make the biggest impact with a single color selection, Holloway says to opt for chartreuse. “We're seeing chartreuse become more welcomed with each passing day. Our clients love the unexpected juxtaposition it creates in a color palette,” she shares. “A punchy Save the Date? A gorgeous table linen? We're here for it. For a recent wedding, our bride kept reiterating she wanted her wedding to feel ‘cool and punchy with a classic twist.’ We incorporated the chartreuse custom pleated table linen with a traditional supper club lamp to create this ‘new traditional’ mix.” Creating a Home Away from Home Expanding on a sense of ease, Weiss says that parties in 2025 are being crafted to look and feel like home, regardless of where they’re actually being hosted. “It’s all about the table settings—we’re designing tables that look like the client’s dining room,” she reveals. “There’s a lot of focus on the details—sometimes we spend an entire week just making tabletop decisions.” The reason? At seated dinners and events, guests spend ample time eating, so the dishes, utensils, glassware, and linens get a lot of face time. “Everyone cares about the dishes and the details,” Weiss points out. “Choosing the right tablecloth, dishes, flatware, glassware, napkin treatment, place card holder—all of these details are big decisions. The good news is the options are no longer limited. Now, there are great companies like Maison de Carine, Casa de Perrin, and Theoni Collection that specialize in really beautiful tabletop pieces you would have in a home. The products don’t look like rentals, and often they are designer brands. Mixing and matching these elements allows hosts to introduce a color theme and style in a very unique and personalized way.” For an event that's smaller in scale, Weiss says to “simply open your cabinets and pull out everything. Things do not need to match. They just need to be part of the same story. Don’t mix fine china with a melamine plate, but mix colors and patterns and create interest. The pieces should be cohesive, but the colors can vary.” Mismatched Glassware When rummaging through your cabinets (or estate sales while hunting for the perfect party setting assortment), Forbes Functions Founder Cameron Forbes says not to worry about selecting a matching set. “In 2025, it’s all about personality, not perfection, and mismatched glassware captures that vibe perfectly,” she reveals. “Mixing vintage finds or colorful stems adds relaxed charm and a unique story to your table. It’s also fun and sustainable to thrift glassware, giving beautiful pieces a second life while cutting down on waste.”
